Risk_Description: Geotechnical report indicates possible rock at P2 depth. Could require blasting or rock breaking increasing cost and duration.
Mitigation_Strategy: Additional geotechnical borings before excavation. Pre-qualify rock excavation subcontractor.
Mitigation_Actions: Completed 4 additional borings in Jan 2025. Rock confirmed at P2. Pre-qualified rock breaker sub.
Contingency_Plan: Shift to mechanical rock breaking. Add $400K contingency for rock excavation.
Actual_Impact: Rock encountered at P2 level. Mechanical breaking added $520K and 4 days to excavation. Managed within contingency.
Notes: Actual cost impact exceeded initial estimate by $120K

Risk_Description: City of Vancouver building permit review may exceed standard timeline due to staff shortages and backlog.
Mitigation_Strategy: Submit permit application early. Engage permit expediter. Pre-consultation with city planning.
Mitigation_Actions: Submitted 2 weeks early. Hired permit expediter in week 2.
Contingency_Plan: Fast-track review application. Escalate to Director of Building.
Actual_Impact: Permit took 30 business days vs planned 25. 5-day delay to critical path.

Risk_Description: Single-source curtain wall supplier may fail to deliver on quality or schedule. Global glass supply chain remains tight.
Mitigation_Strategy: Qualify backup supplier. Include performance bond in facade contract. Require shop drawing submittal 8 weeks early.
Mitigation_Actions: Backup supplier qualified (GlassWorks Pacific). Performance bond secured. Early shop drawings requested.
Contingency_Plan: Switch to backup supplier with unitized system. Accept 3-4 week schedule impact for redesign.
Trigger_Event: Original supplier failed QA in June 2025
Actual_Impact: Original supplier's panels failed thermal cycling QA test. Switched to GlassWorks Pacific unitized system. Redesign took 4 weeks. Delivery delayed 3 weeks beyond revised schedule. Total 21-day critical path impact.
Notes: Largest single risk event on project. Triggered recovery schedule.

Risk_Description: Vancouver market has limited formwork carpenter availability. Multiple high-rise projects competing for same crews.
Mitigation_Strategy: Secure formwork subcontract early with dedicated crew commitment. Include retention incentive clause.
Mitigation_Actions: FormTech Structures contracted with 15-person dedicated crew. Retention bonus at milestone completion.
Contingency_Plan: Bring in out-of-province crew. Accept 1-week delay per occurrence.
Trigger_Event: Crew size drops below 12 for >1 week
Actual_Impact: Brief shortage in Aug 2025 (1 week) when 3 carpenters left for competing project. Resolved with retention bonus activation.

Risk_Description: High-rise construction inherently carries fall risk. A serious incident could trigger WorkSafeBC stop-work order.
Mitigation_Strategy: 100% tie-off policy. Retractable SRL systems on all elevated work. Weekly harness inspections. Monthly safety audits.
Mitigation_Actions: SRL systems installed. Weekly inspections active. Monthly audits by third-party safety consultant.
Contingency_Plan: Full safety stand-down (1 day). WorkSafeBC liaison. Incident investigation. Return-to-work plan.
Actual_Impact: Worker fell 2.5m from formwork on Floor 3 (SAF-009). Fractured wrist and ribs. Full safety stand-down conducted. WorkSafeBC investigation - no stop-work order issued. SRL system upgrade implemented.
Notes: 28 days lost time for injured worker. No project stop-work.

Risk_Description: Vancouver winter (Nov-Feb) brings heavy rain and occasional freezing. Could delay concrete pours and exterior work.
Mitigation_Strategy: Build 5 float days into winter schedule for weather. Procure heated enclosures for concrete curing. Monitor 10-day forecast.
Mitigation_Actions: Heated enclosures procured. Winter schedule includes weather float. Rain gear for all crews.
Contingency_Plan: Shift to weekend/overtime pours during dry windows. Prioritize interior work during extended rain.
Actual_Impact: 3 weather delay days used in Nov-Dec 2025. Within planned float. Heated enclosures enabled concrete work to continue in light rain.

Risk_Description: Dewatering volumes exceeding design predictions. Risk of prolonged dewatering costs and environmental compliance issues.
Mitigation_Strategy: Increase dewatering pump capacity by 30%. Install groundwater monitoring wells. Pre-coordinate with Metro Vancouver on discharge.
Mitigation_Actions: Additional pumps installed. 4 monitoring wells active. Discharge permit secured.
Contingency_Plan: Extend dewatering duration. Add treatment capacity if turbidity exceeds limits.
Trigger_Event: Dewatering rate exceeds 150% of design for >48hrs
Actual_Impact: Water table 0.8m higher than borings indicated. Additional pump managed flow. Sediment discharge incident (SAF-004) triggered filter upgrade.

Risk_Description: Complex mechanical systems in residential floors may conflict with post-tension slab geometry. BIM coordination may not catch all conflicts.
Mitigation_Strategy: Weekly BIM coordination meetings. Mandate LOD 350 models from all subs. Run clash detection biweekly.
Mitigation_Actions: BIM coordination meetings held weekly since Jul 2025. Clash detection runs every 2 weeks. 847 clashes identified and resolved.
Contingency_Plan: Resolve conflicts in field with RFI process. Accept minor cost for field modifications.
Trigger_Event: >10 critical clashes unresolved within 2 weeks of detection
Actual_Impact: No critical clashes have remained unresolved >2 weeks. Field modifications needed for 12 minor clashes not caught in model (total cost ~$35K).
Notes: BIM process working well. See clash dataset for details.

Risk_Title: Material Price Escalation - Steel and Concrete
Risk_Description: Concrete and rebar prices trending upward. Fixed-price subcontracts may not cover full project duration if subs seek price relief.
Mitigation_Strategy: Lock in material pricing through supply agreements where possible. Include escalation clauses capped at 5% in subcontracts.
Mitigation_Actions: Concrete supply agreement locked through Dec 2025. Rebar pricing fixed for full project via Vancouver Rebar Co.
Contingency_Plan: Negotiate partial cost sharing above 5% threshold. Value engineer non-critical elements.
Trigger_Event: Material price index increases >5% in any quarter
Actual_Impact: Concrete prices increased 3.2% in Q3 2025. Rebar stable. Within cap. No additional cost impact to date.

Risk_Description: Construction noise bylaws and stormwater discharge requirements in downtown Vancouver are strict. Non-compliance risks fines and stop-work.
Mitigation_Strategy: Noise monitoring at site boundary. Stormwater management plan. Monthly environmental audit.
Mitigation_Actions: Noise monitors installed. Stormwater plan approved. Monthly audits by EcoGuard Consulting.
Contingency_Plan: Cease offending activity. Engage environmental consultant. Self-report to authorities.
Trigger_Event: Noise complaint from adjacent residents OR discharge exceedance detected
Actual_Impact: Sediment discharge incident in Mar 2025 (SAF-004) was resolved before regulatory involvement. 2 noise complaints received - resolved by adjusting pile driving hours.

Risk_Title: Schedule Recovery Failure - Facade Delay Cascade
Risk_Description: 21-day facade delay may cascade further if curtain wall installation rate does not meet recovery schedule.
Mitigation_Strategy: Increase curtain wall crew from 6 to 10 installers. Add Saturday shifts for facade work. Track daily installation rate.
Mitigation_Actions: Crew increased to 10 in Jan 2026. Saturday shifts active since Feb 2026. Daily progress tracked.
Contingency_Plan: Accept compressed commissioning schedule. Negotiate partial occupancy permit for lower floors.
Trigger_Event: Installation rate falls below 1 floor per 10 working days
Actual_Impact: Crew of 10 achieving 1 floor per 9 days - on revised recovery schedule. Fl 17-25 at 65% as of Mar 31. Risk of further delay remains if any disruption occurs.

Risk_Description: Multiple early risks (rock excavation + facade redesign) have consumed significant contingency. Remaining contingency may be insufficient for closeout phase risks.
Mitigation_Strategy: Monthly contingency tracking. Prioritize remaining contingency for commissioning and deficiency phases. Value engineer where possible.
Mitigation_Actions: Original contingency: $4.1M (5%). Spent to date: $3.2M. Remaining: $900K for 3 months.
Contingency_Plan: Request owner approval for additional contingency. Identify VE opportunities in landscaping and common areas.
Actual_Impact: Contingency burn rate is a concern. $900K remaining for commissioning and closeout phases. Two active VE items under review ($180K potential savings).
Notes: Discussed with owner at March progress meeting

Risk_Title: Confined Space Incidents - Elevator Shaft and Mechanical Rooms
Risk_Description: Elevator shaft and mechanical rooms present confined space hazards during installation and commissioning phases.
Mitigation_Strategy: Confined space entry permits for all shaft and mechanical room work. Continuous air monitoring. Rescue plan and trained rescue team on site.
Mitigation_Actions: Entry permits active. Air monitors deployed. 3 workers trained as rescue team.
Contingency_Plan: Stop work in affected area. Deploy rescue team. Report to WorkSafeBC if entry rescue required.
Trigger_Event: Any confined space atmospheric reading outside safe parameters
Actual_Impact: No confined space incidents to date. 45 safe entries completed.

Risk_Description: Downtown location adjacent to hotel and residential tower. Construction noise vibration and truck traffic may generate complaints and political pressure.
Mitigation_Strategy: Community liaison program. Monthly newsletter to neighbours. Designated complaint hotline. Vibration monitoring on adjacent structures.
Mitigation_Actions: Liaison program active. 6 newsletters issued. Hotline staffed Mon-Sat.
Contingency_Plan: Adjust construction hours. Meet with complainants. Engage city councillor's office if escalated.
Trigger_Event: >3 formal complaints in any month OR complaint from city councillor's office
Actual_Impact: 12 total complaints received over 15 months. Peak was 4 in one month during piling (Apr 2025). Resolved through adjusted working hours and direct communication.

Risk_Description: Building automation system integration with HVAC and fire protection may reveal sequencing or communication errors not detectable until full system testing.
Mitigation_Strategy: Phase commissioning by system. Pre-functional testing before integrated testing. Require BAS vendor on-site during commissioning week.
Mitigation_Actions: Pre-functional tests scheduled for Apr 2026. BAS vendor (Siemens) committed to 2-week on-site presence.
Contingency_Plan: Extend commissioning by 1 week. Bring in BAS specialist troubleshooting team.
Actual_Impact: Commissioning not yet started. Pre-functional test protocols under review. BAS install at 70% - on track for April start.
Notes: Key risk for final project phase
